text:
Setting clear project goals is essential for success. Using the SMART framework helps ensure your goals are effective. SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," set a goal like "increase website traffic by 20% within three months."

Once you have your main goals, break them down into smaller milestones. These are key checkpoints that help you track progress. Each milestone should lead to a clear deliverable, such as a completed report or a new feature. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. Make sure everyone involved, from team members to clients, agrees on the goals and milestones. This keeps the project focused and avoids confusion later on.
